ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0KL covers tea-based beverages; no specific bottled iced tea brand code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229969007 is the closest available concept for iced tea; no brand-specific code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard retail bottle of Yuanqi Forest Iced Tea is approximately 480 ml, equivalent to 480 g, which is a typical single-serve portion sold in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, tea, ready-to-drink, sweetened — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Branded Foods; cross-referenced with Yuanqi Forest product label data (low-sugar iced tea variant)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Yuanqi Forest Iced Tea is a Chinese branded RTD tea. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for ready-to-drink sweetened tea (FDC ID 174926) and cross-referenced with publicly available Yuanqi Forest product label information indicating approximately 4–5 g sugar per 100 ml and near-zero protein and fat. Values represent a lightly sweetened bottled iced tea profile.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweetened ready-to-drink tea beverages — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened iced tea
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Yuanqi Forest Iced Tea. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for lightly sweetened ready-to-drink tea beverages, which typically range from 30–40. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (35 × 24.0) / 100 = 8.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~5 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 5 mg sodium per 100 ml, Yuanqi Forest Iced Tea poses minimal sodium concern for individuals managing hypertension, though total daily sugar intake from sweetened beverages should still be monitored.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content (~4.5 g per 100 ml) contributing to glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the glycemic index is low (~35), the added sugar in this lightly sweetened bottled tea contributes meaningful glycemic load across a full 480 ml serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size or choose the zero-sugar variant.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and sodium; moderate added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Yuanqi Forest Iced Tea contains no fat or cholesterol and very low sodium, making it a low cardiovascular risk beverage, though regular consumption of added-sugar drinks should be minimised in line with cardiovascular dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yuanqi Forest Iced Tea (元气森林冰茶) is a popular Chinese-brand ready-to-drink bottled iced tea sold widely in Singapore and across Asia. It is brewed from tea extract, lightly sweetened with sugar, and flavoured with natural ingredients, resulting in a refreshing, mildly sweet, and slightly astringent beverage. The product is typically sold in 480 ml plastic bottles and is consumed cold as a standalone drink or alongside meals. Nutritionally, it is a low-calorie beverage providing approximately 20 kcal per 100 ml, with negligible protein and fat. The carbohydrate content is approximately 5 g per 100 ml, primarily from added sugar (~4.5 g). Sodium is very low at approximately 5 mg per 100 ml. The glycemic index is estimated at 35 (low), making it a lower-GI choice among sweetened beverages, though the glycemic load across a full bottle is moderate. It is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following halal diets. Clinically, it is low in sodium and fat, posing minimal concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease, but the added sugar content warrants moderation for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The Yuanqi Forest brand is known for its 'sparkling water' and 'light sugar' product lines, and this iced tea variant is part of their mainstream RTD tea range.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for comparable ready-to-drink sweetened tea and cross-referenced with publicly available Yuanqi Forest product label information. No direct entry for this specific branded product exists in an approved primary regional source (HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods with full nutrient panel). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for lightly sweetened RTD teas. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. protein_g is confirmed zero: this is a tea-based beverage with no protein-containing ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central RTD tea reference. 2. fat_g is confirmed zero: no fat-containing ingredients are present in this beverage formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central RTD tea reference. 3. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero: beverages of this type contain no dietary fibre. 4. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this beverage category. 5. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this beverage category. 6. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 7. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported. 8. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 9. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 10. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 11. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 12. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. 13. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported. 14. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. 15. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 16. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 17. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 18. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 19. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. 20. The food_name in the input ('Ice Tea元气森林') has been normalised to 'Iced Tea (Yuanqi Forest Brand)' as the canonical English name. 21. Multilingual naming follows Singaporean dish rules given the Singapore sales context.
